subject: Economics
chapter: Employment: Growth, Informalisation and Other Issues

cases.passage: The Indian government has launched a massive employment generation scheme, 'MGNREGA', to provide 100 days of guaranteed employment to rural households. This scheme aims to reduce unemployment and poverty in rural areas. However, despite its good intentions, the scheme has been criticized for creating a culture of dependency among the beneficiaries. Many have pointed out that the scheme has also led to a decline in the quality of work and an increase in wages, making it difficult for other employers to compete.

questions.q: What is the main objective of the 'MGNREGA' scheme?
opts: (A) To reduce poverty and unemployment in urban areas
(B) To provide 100 days of guaranteed employment to rural households
(C) To increase wages and improve working conditions for all employers
(D) To reduce government expenditure on employment schemes
questions.explanation: The scheme aims to provide 100 days of guaranteed employment to rural households.

questions.q: What has been a criticism of the 'MGNREGA' scheme?
opts: (A) It has led to a decrease in government expenditure on employment schemes
(B) It has created a culture of dependency among the beneficiaries
(C) It has improved the quality of work and reduced wages
(D) It has increased employment opportunities in urban areas
questions.explanation: Many have pointed out that the scheme has created a culture of dependency among the beneficiaries.

questions.q: What has been an impact of the 'MGNREGA' scheme on other employers?
opts: (A) It has increased their ability to compete with government-provided jobs
(B) It has decreased their ability to compete with government-provided jobs
(C) It has had no impact on their ability to compete
(D) It has increased their wages and improved working conditions
questions.explanation: The scheme has led to a decline in the quality of work and an increase in wages, making it difficult for other employers to compete.

questions.q: What type of area does the 'MGNREGA' scheme target?
opts: (A) Urban areas
(B) Rural areas
(C) Both urban and rural areas
(D) Only specific regions
questions.explanation: The scheme aims to provide employment to rural households.

questions.q: What is the duration of employment guaranteed by the 'MGNREGA' scheme?
opts: (A) 50 days
(B) 100 days
(C) 150 days
(D) 200 days
questions.explanation: The scheme guarantees 100 days of employment to rural households.

cases.passage: The informal sector in India has been growing rapidly, with many workers shifting from formal employment to informal employment. This shift is due to various reasons such as lack of job security, low wages, and poor working conditions in the formal sector. Informal workers, on the other hand, face challenges such as lack of social security, no access to benefits, and exploitation by employers. Despite these challenges, many workers prefer informal employment due to its flexibility and ease of entry.

questions.q: Why do workers shift from formal to informal employment?
opts: (A) Due to lack of job security and low wages in the formal sector
(B) Due to better working conditions and higher wages in the formal sector
(C) Due to lack of flexibility and ease of entry in the formal sector
(D) Due to lack of social security and no access to benefits in the informal sector
questions.explanation: Workers shift from formal to informal employment due to lack of job security, low wages, and poor working conditions in the formal sector.

questions.q: What are some challenges faced by informal workers?
opts: (A) Lack of job security and low wages
(B) Lack of social security, no access to benefits, and exploitation by employers
(C) Poor working conditions and lack of flexibility
(D) Higher wages and better working conditions
questions.explanation: Informal workers face challenges such as lack of social security, no access to benefits, and exploitation by employers.

questions.q: Why do many workers prefer informal employment?
opts: (A) Due to lack of flexibility and ease of entry
(B) Due to better working conditions and higher wages
(C) Due to its flexibility and ease of entry
(D) Due to lack of social security and no access to benefits
questions.explanation: Many workers prefer informal employment due to its flexibility and ease of entry.

questions.q: What is a characteristic of informal employment?
opts: (A) Lack of job security and low wages
(B) Lack of flexibility and ease of entry
(C) Flexibility and ease of entry
(D) Better working conditions and higher wages
questions.explanation: Informal employment is characterized by its flexibility and ease of entry.

questions.q: What is a reason for the growth of the informal sector in India?
opts: (A) Increase in job security and higher wages in the formal sector
(B) Decrease in flexibility and ease of entry in the informal sector
(C) Increase in lack of job security and low wages in the formal sector
(D) Decrease in lack of social security and no access to benefits in the informal sector
questions.explanation: The informal sector is growing due to various reasons such as lack of job security, low wages, and poor working conditions in the formal sector.
